title: SkyDeck.ai for Entrepreneurs — Always-On AI Agents, Controlled Spend
description: Give a small team the leverage of always-on AI agents in Agent Pods — build with Claude Code, do founder work in GenStudio, and keep burn predictable with spend guardrails.
lede: Run Claude Code, OpenClaw, and custom agents around the clock in Agent Pods, do the founder work in GenStudio, and keep burn predictable with Control Center spend guardrails.
html:
Give a small team the leverage of always-on agents — without a big headcount or a runaway bill.

Run like a bigger team

Founders wear every hat. SkyDeck.ai lets you hand the repetitive ones to agents that keep working after you log off. Launch an Agent Pod — a persistent cloud workspace — drop in Claude Code, OpenClaw, or a custom agent, and let it run around the clock. The main terminal survives disconnects, idle pods auto-sleep to save money, and everything resumes exactly where it stopped. One person can keep several agents moving at once, which is the closest thing a startup has to extra hands.

  • Put a long build, a research pass, or an ops job on an agent overnight.
  • Keep one workspace for chat, prompts, and tools in GenStudio.
  • Watch spend from day one — before it ever becomes a problem.
  • Reach every major model through one interface, with no vendor to lock you in.

Build the product with an agent in the loop

If you are shipping software, an Agent Pod is where your coding agent lives. Run a Claude Code session that keeps going through a long task, preview the app in a built-in browser pane on the pod's exposed ports, and come back to finished work. Everything in /workspace persists across sessions and Claude Code pods keep ~/.claude, so your project and credentials are there when you resume.

  • Build around the clock — a long task keeps running after you close the laptop.
  • Preview as you go — see the app in the pod's browser pane while the agent changes it.
  • Nothing lost — resume the pod and pick up the work, not a fresh setup.

Validate the market and run the business

Before and beyond the product, GenStudio gives you any model in one place to do the founder work: size a market, map competitors, draft a pitch, or turn customer notes into a plan. Share the prompts that work with a co-founder or your first hires, and schedule the recurring jobs — a weekly competitive scan, a standing summary — so they run without you and land where you already work.

  • Market and competitive research — gather and weigh the signal you need to make the call.
  • Positioning and pitch — sharpen the story until it lands with customers and investors.
  • Operations on autopilot — schedule the routine work and get the results in Slack or email.

Control burn — because it is your money

For a startup, predictable spend is survival. The Control Center puts a hard frame around it: set a monthly budget and choose to block, warn, or allow overage; cap spend per member as you add your first hires; limit how many pods run at once; and let idle pods sleep on their own. Credit is metered per token with no lock-in, so you pay for what you use and no more. For a company counting every dollar of runway, that is the difference between adopting AI and worrying about it. Read the Agent Pods guide, or see it in context on the Agent Pods page.

Grow without switching tools

Start solo and add teammates into the same workspace as you hire. One Driver operates an agent at a time while others observe live, so your first engineers pair inside the same terminal instead of over a screen-share — and you reach every major model through one interface, with no vendor to lock you in as you scale. The workspace that carried you at one person is the same one that carries a team of ten.

Get started
